Welcome to NEXUS

A single platform for network devices, updates, configurations, command workflows, and connected operational systems.

Current Announcements

Unified Network Operations Platform

NEXUS brings generation, validation, field execution, maintenance, and operational tooling into one workspace so teams can move across network workflows without losing context.

Use the generators, device tools, command workflows, and connected operational systems from the navigation to move between planning, execution, and support in one place.

MikroTik Config Generator

This tool generates configuration files, along with corresponding port maps, for MikroTik routers. To use the tool, select the config type, then either upload a JSON/CSV file containing parameters, or enter manually. At least one backhaul is required to generate a configuration file.

View Policy Reference for port assignments and configuration details.

Tower Configuration Generator

Backend-driven form for MikroTik tower configs. Legacy tower form sections are hidden.

BNG2 Parameters

Feature Subnets

Tarana Sector Ports

Assign each sector to a physical port. Port names auto-adapt to your router model.

Backhauls

Switch Uplinks (Policy: sfp-sfpplus1-2)

New Device: Fill form below, click Generate. Tenant defaults will be auto-loaded.
Drag & Drop Config File
or click to browse (.rsc or .txt)
From old device: /export file=backup.rsc

SSH Device Connection

Note: This will SSH into the device and run /export command to fetch the current configuration.
Comma-separated. Default is 22, then 5022. You can add custom ports.
Loaded:
Leave this off for normal Nextlink sites so migration stays on `sfp28-*` ports only.

Translated Configuration

✅ IP Validation Summary
Source IPs: 0 Translated IPs: 0
⚠ Missing IPs (0):
🛈 Compliance-Replaced IPs (0):
These IPs were in source SNMP/firewall address-lists that the compliance template replaces. This is expected behavior.
🛈 Port-Exhaustion IPs (0):
Target device has fewer ports; these IPs are documented in PORT-EXHAUSTION comments.
✔ All source IPs preserved in translated config.

Configuration Type

Leave unchecked to avoid accidentally disabling live uplinks/backhauls not explicitly configured in the form.
Note: Configuration type determines which features are available and automatically configured.

Site Information

Device Selection

Routing (OSPF/BGP - Uses Router ID)

Optional Features

Tarana Sector Configuration Generator

Router IP = first usable in the subnet.
(Tarana fragment included automatically in the full generated config when 'Enable Tarana Ports' is checked)
Note: This generator creates the bridge, ethernet sets, VLAN interfaces (1000/2000/3000), bridge-port mappings and a management IP on UNICORNMGMT plus a basic OSPF interface-template entry.

DHCP Configuration (backbone-v2 BGP/OSPF Areas Only)

Note: DHCP is only used in backbone-v2 areas with BGP/OSPF. Other areas (MPLS/VPLS) use BNGs for subscriber management.

VPLS Configuration

Configure VPLS tunnels for BNG connectivity.

Port & Uplink Configuration

Configure ports with IP addresses for OSPF routing, bridge assignment, or switch mode. The tool automatically handles OSPF templates, IP addressing, and MPLS LDP configuration.

Auto-Configuration: When you configure ports:
  • Routed (OSPF): Automatically assigns IP, creates OSPF interface template with correct network matching the IP, includes in MPLS LDP (if enabled)
  • Bridge: Assigns port to selected bridge, configures bridge port settings
  • Switch: Configures as switch port (no OSPF, no IP)
Note: Unused SFP ports will be automatically disabled for security.

VLAN Interfaces

Create VLAN interfaces on physical interfaces.

MPLS/LDP Configuration

Backhaul Uplinks (IPs auto to ports/OSPF) - DEPRECATED: Use Port & Uplink Configuration above

Up to 4; generates ethernet sets, /ip address, OSPF templates (ptp type). Uses sfp4+ downwards.

Auto-Configuration: When you add uplinks here, the tool will automatically:
  • Assign IP addresses to the ports
  • Configure OSPF interface templates with proper authentication
  • Include in MPLS LDP configuration (if MPLS enabled)
Note: Interface speed and comments are configured in the "Port Configuration" section below to avoid duplication.

Static Customer Management

Add static customer IP ranges that will be automatically included in firewall address lists for proper routing and access control.

Note: Static customers will be automatically added to firewall address lists for proper routing and access control.

6GHz Configuration Generator

Policy-compliant 6GHz switch configuration with VLAN 3000/4000, standardized port assignments, bonding, and DHCP pools.

View Policy Reference for port assignments and configuration details.

6GHz Switch Configuration (IN-STATE)

Switch type determines port naming and bonding configuration
6GHz management subnet
6GHz CPE subnet
0 = GW+1; increase to reserve more in front

6GHz Switch Configuration (OUT-OF-STATE)

Simple bridge and VLAN configuration for out-of-state deployments.

Switch type determines port naming
First port for bonding
Second port for bonding
Single port for MT309 (no bonding)

Nokia Configurator

Generate Nokia transport configurations from one workspace. 7250 includes both classic hierarchy and command-line output formats, and this same page now expands for 7210, 7750, BNG 2.0, and ISD profiles.

Platform Selection

Enterprise generation uses this single device selector as the source of truth for uplink, customer handoff, and NAT port placement.
7250 Standard is fully available on this page today. Other Nokia profiles are being folded into this same configurator instead of split into separate disconnected tools.

System Information

Typing the short site name auto-fills the full Nokia system name as RTR-NK7250-AR1.<SITE-NAME>.

Security & Credentials

Secure: Credentials are loaded from the server environment at generate-time. They are never embedded in the page.

SNMP Community •••••
nlroot Password •••••
admin Password •••••
BGP Auth Key •••••

Service Configuration

7210 Routing Profile

7210 ISD Service Profile

7750 Tunnel / VPLS Profile

100G Connectors

High-speed fiber connections. Each connector creates a breakout port (e.g., 1/1/c23/1).

ConnectorDescriptionIP/CIDRMTU

Uplinks

Standard uplink ports for microwave, switch, and NNI connections.

PortDescriptionIP/CIDRSpeedMTU

LDP Deny Prefix List

Prefixes to deny in LDP filter policy. One per line.

BGP Neighbors

Default profile: Uses tenant route-reflector peers and tenant ASN.

Group NameNeighbor IP

③ Nokia Settings & Overrides

Auto-generated from MikroTik identity — editable
Choose whether to keep the existing MikroTik loopback (loop0) IP or assign a new IP for the Nokia 7250. This IP becomes the Nokia system interface, router-id, and is used for OSPF, BGP, MPLS, RSVP, and LDP across all state profiles.
Enter in CIDR format (e.g., 10.42.12.88/32). This will replace the MikroTik loopback in all routing sections.

④ Nokia SR OS Configuration

FTTH BNG Configuration Generator

Generate MikroTik CCR2216 configurations for FTTH OLT deployments

Router Information

Router identity will be auto-generated

IP Allocations

Required Subnets:
  • Loopback: /32 (e.g., 10.26.1.111/32)
  • CPE Network: /22 (e.g., 10.30.100.0/22)
  • CGNAT Private: /22 (e.g., 100.80.108.0/22)
  • CGNAT Public: /32 (e.g., 52.128.63.173/32)
  • UNAUTH Network: /22 (e.g., 10.130.100.0/22)
  • OLT Network 1: /29 (e.g., 10.246.46.88/29)
  • OLT Network 2: /29 (Optional, e.g., 10.25.251.24/29)

BGP Peer Configuration

FTTH BGP uses the loopback IP as `local.address` and `router-id`. The backend automatically builds the core peers below.

CR7
Tenant peer 1
CR8
Tenant peer 2

Port & Uplink Configuration

Configure ports with IP addresses for OSPF routing, bridge assignment, or switch mode. The tool automatically handles OSPF templates, IP addressing, and MPLS LDP configuration.

OLT LAG PORTS CONFIGURATION

Configure LAG ports for each OLT. Use LAG 1 for sfp28-3,4,5,6. LAG 2 is optional for sfp28-7,8,9,10.

Command Vault

Quick command references for day-to-day NOC operations.

Nokia 7750 - BNG Commands Guide

Common commands with quick copy.

Lookup IPOE Session/SAP/Plan/IP/Session Type

MAC must be lowercase.

/show service id 300 ipoe session detail mac <mac>

Lookup Lease by MAC

Useful for DHCP lease verification in service 300.

/show service id 300 dhcp lease-state mac <mac>

Clear Subscriber IPOE Session

Requires subscriber SAP and MAC. No output after a successful clear.

/clear service id 300 ipoe session sap <sap> mac <mac>

Verify SDP Tunnels under BNG VPLS

Examples: 1245, 1249, 4245, 4249.

/show service id <service-id> sdp

Verify STP for BNG VPLS

Check bridge state and role quickly.

/show service id <service-id> stp

View Speed Plan Count per Subscriber Profile

Shows SLA profile counters.

/show subscriber-mgmt statistics sla-profiles

Nokia 7250 - Port Setup

Generate per-port configuration commands for Nokia 7250 IXR (classic CLI).

Ports to Configure

Port Description (Interface Name) IP / CIDR MTU Speed No Shut

Cisco Command Vault - IOS/XE Quick Guide

Common troubleshooting and verification commands.

Interface Status Overview

Quick health check for all interfaces.

show interfaces status

Interface Error Counters

Use for CRC/input/output error investigation.

show interfaces <interface>

BGP Neighbor Summary

Checks BGP session state and prefixes.

show ip bgp summary

OSPF Neighbor Check

Confirm adjacency and state.

show ip ospf neighbor

Routing Table Lookup

Verify route selection and next hop.

show ip route <prefix>

ARP / MAC Correlation

Map endpoint IP-to-MAC and MAC-to-port.

show ip arp | include <ip-or-mac> show mac address-table | include <mac>

Backhaul Interface Template

Starting template for a routed backhaul handoff.

configure terminal interface TenGigE 0/0/0/1 description BH-TO-SITE ipv4 address 10.10.10.1 255.255.255.252

OSPF PtP Template

Common interface-level OSPF settings for a transport link.

router ospf 1 area 0 interface TenGigE 0/0/0/1 cost 10 authentication message-digest message-digest-key 1 md5 encrypted 0456532B5A0B5B580D2028 network point-to-point no passive

MikroTik Command Vault - RouterOS Quick Guide

High-use RouterOS commands for NOC troubleshooting, grouped by hierarchy.

Hierarchy Map (Top-Level Paths)

/interface * Physical and logical interfaces: ethernet, bridge, vlan, bonding, vpls, bridge ports.
/ip * Layer-3 and services: addressing, routes, DNS, ARP, IP services, DHCP and helper tables.
/routing * Control-plane protocols and templates: BGP peers/templates, OSPF instances/areas/interfaces.
/mpls * MPLS forwarding and LDP behavior: interfaces, LDP config, accept/advertise filters.
/ip firewall * Policy enforcement and traffic handling: filter, NAT, raw, mangle, address-list, service-port.
/system * System identity, clock/NTP, logging, watchdog, notes, and routerboard platform settings.
/snmp * SNMP runtime and community controls for polling and trap source behavior.
/user * User accounts, RBAC groups, auth model (`/user aaa`), and privilege policy controls.
/tool * Operational tooling including RoMON, packet capture/sniffer, troubleshooting and diagnostics.
/radius * RADIUS servers, shared secrets, source-address, and service binding (dhcp/login).

Operational Commands

Interface Status and Traffic

Live status/throughput view per interface.

/interface print terse /interface monitor-traffic <interface>

BGP Session State

RouterOS 7 BGP session summary.

/routing bgp session print detail

OSPF Neighbor State

Verify adjacency and interface participation.

/routing ospf neighbor print detail

Route Lookup

Check selected route and distance.

/ip route print detail where dst-address~"<prefix>"

ARP and DHCP Lease Lookup

Find endpoint by MAC or IP quickly.

/ip arp print where address=<ip> or mac-address=<mac> /ip dhcp-server lease print where address=<ip> or mac-address=<mac>

RoMON (Router Management Overlay)

Out-of-band L2 management plane between MikroTik devices.

/tool romon print /tool romon set enabled=yes /tool romon port print

Firewall Policy Snapshot

Quickly audit filter/NAT/raw/mangle rule sets.

/ip firewall filter print detail /ip firewall nat print detail /ip firewall raw print detail /ip firewall mangle print detail

MPLS/LDP Verification

Validate LDP state and filter posture.

/mpls ldp print detail /mpls ldp interface print detail /mpls ldp accept-filter print detail /mpls ldp advertise-filter print detail

SNMP Runtime and Communities

Confirm polling source and community setup.

/snmp print detail /snmp community print detail

User / Group / AAA Audit

Review account posture and access model quickly.

/user print detail /user group print detail /user aaa print

Recent System Logs

Filter log output for failures quickly.

/log print where message~"error|critical|warning"

 Policy Reference

                
            

            

                
This tool automatically follows your configured network policy for all generated
                        configurations.


                

                    
                    

                        
 Tower Config Policy

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             sfp-sfpplus1-2 = Switches

                             sfp-sfpplus4+ = Backhaul links

                             sfp-sfpplus6 = LTE gear (if present)

                             sfp-sfpplus6-8 = Tarana gear (if present)


                            Features:

                             v6  v7 migration

                             CCR2004 & CCR1036 support

                             MPLS/VPLS & Non-MPLS towers

                             BGP/OSPF routing protocols

                             Standardized security policies
                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
 Non-MPLS Enterprise Policy
                        

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             ether5-12 = Local services (RPC, switches)

                             ether7-12 = Customer access

                             sfp-sfpplus1-2 = Switches

                             sfp-sfpplus4+ = Backhaul links


                            Features:

                             Bridge-based customer access

                             DHCP pools for customers

                             NAT for internet access

                             No MPLS/VPLS configuration

                             Standard IP routing only
                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
 MPLS Enterprise Policy

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             ether5-12 = Local services (RPC, switches)

                             sfp-sfpplus1-2 = Switches

                             sfp-sfpplus4+ = Backhaul links

                             sfp-sfpplus6 = LTE gear (if present)


                            Features:

                             OSPF/BGP routing protocols

                             MPLS/LDP configuration

                             VPLS tunnels for customers

                             RouterOS v6/v7 compatibility

                             Standardized security policies
                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
 FTTH BNG Policy

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             sfp28-1 = Core uplink (routed/OSPF)

                             sfp28-3/4/5/6 = OLT LAG 1

                             sfp28-7/8/9/10 = OLT LAG 2 (optional)


                            Features:

                             LAG-based OLT uplinks and VLAN bridges

                             DHCP pools for CPE/UNAUTH/CGNAT

                             BGP/OSPF/MPLS baseline templates

                             Standardized firewall and logging
                        

                    


                    
                    
                    
                    

                        
 Tarana Policy (BNG1)

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             sfp-sfpplus6-8 = Tarana gear

                             sfp-sfpplus1-2 = Switches

                             sfp-sfpplus4+ = Backhaul links

                             UNICORNMGMT = Bridge interface


                            Sectors:

                             ALPHA = Primary sector

                             BETA = Secondary sector

                             GAMMA = Tertiary sector

                             DELTA = Quaternary sector

                             Bridge-based management
                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
 Tarana Policy (BNG2 - Out of
                            State)

                        

                            Bridge Assignments:

                             bridge3000 = Management (VLAN 3000)

                             bridge1000 = VLAN 1000

                             bridge2000 = VLAN 2000

                             VLANs: 1000, 2000, 3000 only


                            Sectors:

                             ALPHA = Primary sector

                             BETA = Secondary sector

                             GAMMA = Tertiary sector

                             DELTA = Quaternary sector (Optional - 4 sectors)
                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
 Enterprise Feeding Policy
                        

                        

                            Port Assignments:

                             ether1 = Management (ALWAYS)

                             ether5-12 = Local services

                             sfp-sfpplus1-2 = Switches

                             sfp-sfpplus4+ = Backhaul links

                             Customer-facing ports = Uplinks


                            Features:

                             Commercial uplink provisioning

                             Enterprise customer access

                             Standardized security policies

                             RouterOS v6/v7 compatibility

                             Bridge-based customer access
                        

                    

                


                

                    
General Policy Principles

                    
    
                        
  • Device Port Assignments: Based on RouterBoard model and configured policy standards
                        
    • 
                        
  • IP Address Management: Dynamic calculation following configured policy conventions
                        
    • 
                        
  • OSPF/BGP Configuration: Compliant with configured routing policies
    • 
                        
  • Firewall Rules: Standard security baselines
    • 
                        
  • DHCP Pools: Calculated dynamically based on subnet size
    • 
                        
  • VLAN Assignments: Following configured VLAN standards
    • 
                        
  • MPLS/VPLS: Configured per your enterprise policy set
    • 
                    

                


                

                    For detailed policy documentation, see: NEXTLINK_INTERNET_POLICY.md
